title: IAM, Bucket Policies, Access Points, and Tenant Isolation · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A TypeScript pairing room incrementally builds least-privilege TypeScript authorization proof, validates one changed runtime input, and keeps earlier HTTP, accessibility, security, stream, AbortSignal, and resource-budget rules active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of least-privilege TypeScript authorization proof while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, enforce Block Public Access and bucket-owner-enforced Object Ownership while explaining what each does and does not authorize.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, derive tenant-safe keys and list prefixes server-side and bind allowed actions and conditions without trusting user-supplied policy fragments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Principal Action Resource Con…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 5016 during a private media entitlement change. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to evaluate a runtime-validated explicit-deny-aware tenant authorization record; retain keyboard and screen-reader progress, cancellation, error, and recovery evidence; then change object version, signed header, or cache dimension and reject stale authority. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Principal Action Resource Con….
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Principal Action Resource Con…, keep Principal Action Resource Condition connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Amazon S3 Security Best Practices — Public block, least privilege, ownership, encryption, logging, monitoring, and detective controls.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Policy Simulation Negative Tests · changed case · Workshop Policy Si…, name the stakeholder, unknown schema, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, promise, stream, cache, cost, abort, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Policy Simulation Negative Tests precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Policy Simulation Negative Tests · changed case · Workshop Policy Si…, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 1869 during a regional recovery and budget exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to evaluate a runtime-validated explicit-deny-aware tenant authorization record; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, review effective permissions with policy analysis, simulation, denied changed cases, temporary credentials, and CloudTrail evidence.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Policy Simulation Negative Tests · changed case · Workshop Policy Si…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: One successful authorized request proves there is no broader unintended access. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Policy Simulation Negative…
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-policy-simulation-negative-tests-x84j9d" that uses case 1958 to evaluate a runtime-validated explicit-deny-aware tenant authorization record. Guided rehearsal: predict the branch, make one bounded increment, and inspect peer-readable changed output. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Policy Simulation Negative….
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Policy Simulation Negative Tests into observable performance evidence. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Policy Simulation Negative…, keep Policy Simulation Negative Tests connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.
